lines: -            // XML.toJSONObject(..., keepStrings=true) historically preserved all tag values
-            // as strings. Only treat "null" as JSONObject.NULL when coercion is enabled.

input.description: Show exit code from Maven test run

content.text: Fixed XML parsing regression for `keepStrings` where `<t>null</t>` was being coerced to JSON null. - Root cause: `XML.parse()` always called `XML.stringToValue()` for non-boolean/non-number tokens, which converts `"null"` to `JSONObject.NULL`, even when `keepStrings` is enabled. - Fix: In `org.json.XML`, when `config.keepStrings` is true, bypass `stringToValue()` and accumulate the raw string for both attributes and tag text content. - Validation: Added a JUnit regression test reproducing the issue, confirmed it fails before the fix and passes after; ran full `mvn test` successfully; then deleted the temporary test file as required.

trajectory.result: Fixed XML parsing regression for `keepStrings` where `<t>null</t>` was being coerced to JSON null. - Root cause: `XML.parse()` always called `XML.stringToValue()` for non-boolean/non-number tokens, which converts `"null"` to `JSONObject.NULL`, even when `keepStrings` is enabled. - Fix: In `org.json.XML`, when `config.keepStrings` is true, bypass `stringToValue()` and accumulate the raw string for both attributes and tag text content. - Validation: Added a JUnit regression test reproducing the issue, confirmed it fails before the fix and passes after; ran full `mvn test` successfully; then deleted the temporary test file as required.
